title: Remove Boilerplate
documentation:
## Purpose
A boolean flag that tells a machine to strip standard boilerplate content from its input or output — such as legal disclaimers, headers, footers, or repeated standard clauses — before further processing.

## When to use
Enable when processing documents that contain recurring non-informative content that would consume token budget without adding analytical value (e.g., contract preambles, cookie banners, standard license headers).

## Notes
The definition of "boilerplate" is machine-specific. See [Include Comments Flag](https://capdag.com/browse/media:include-comments-flag;textable) for the analogous control over code comments and [Preserve Structure Flag](https://capdag.com/browse/media:preserve-structure-flag;textable) for retaining document structure.
